data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Google's architecture leverages a microservices approach that allows for scalability across its various services.
data.technical_spec: Uses containers managed by Kubernetes for deploying individual microservices.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Efficient load balancing across thousands of servers ensures high availability.
data.technical_spec: Globally distributed load balancers handle traffic distribution.

data.title: Global CDN
data.description: Content Delivery Networks are used to deliver content through edge locations.
data.technical_spec: Employs extensive use of CDNs for latency reduction.

data.title: Data Center Efficiency
data.description: Optimized data centers with custom hardware to increase energy efficiency.
data.technical_spec: Uses TPUs and custom server designs.

data.title: Distributed File System
data.description: GFS (Google File System) stores and manages large amounts of data efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Highly scalable file storage for handling vast amounts of data.
